items.prompt: A workflow file in tools such as Snakemake or Nextflow primarily:
choices: Captures ordered pipeline steps and dependencies so runs stay consistent
Replaces the need for any dataset documentation
Deletes container images after every local notebook cell
items.explain: Example 13.29 and Section 13.7 emphasize capturing the full procedure in a workflow definition.

items.prompt: A Jupyter notebook can combine loading, cleaning, modeling, and plotting with narrative documentation in one literate document.
items.explain: Example 13.31 describes a notebook pipeline from load through visualization with decisions documented beside code.

items.prompt: Pinning BWA and Samtools versions inside a Docker image mainly solves:
choices: Environment and tool-version drift across machines
items.explain: Example 13.34 uses Docker to pin sequence-alignment tool versions for consistent runs.

items.prompt: Name one library family that Example 13.36 might pin inside an ML training Docker image.
items.rubric: Accept TensorFlow, NumPy, pandas, or equivalent training-stack libraries from the example.

items.prompt: Containers help reduce “it works on my machine” failures by sharing a consistent computational environment.
items.explain: Section 13.7 emphasizes Docker as a way to keep environments consistent across collaborators and platforms.
